description: next/ v4 — PHASE-0 ARCHITECTURE FIX: REAL ERP OperationsResponse (Level-3 IST/Rückmeldung) via the sim-v5 ERP REST API (http://192.168.178.154:38260/api/confirmations), replacing the postgresql/erp-operations-response.json direct-erpdb poller (erpdb.llm_test_v3.order_confirmations — the forbidden direct-DB bypass). Same sourceId (edge SOURCE_IDS unchanged), same profile contract (SMProfile-OperationsResponse). production_order_no = join key (cross-rule join + kgIdProperty; RESPONDS_TO ProductionOrder on the same key). confirmed_qty -> quantity_produced drives the qty_shortfall cross-rule against ProductionOrder.qty_planned. cycle_time_actual -> cycle_time_actual_sec (the real Ist cycle time). ts_end -> actual_end_time + completed_at (the REST confirmation's end timestamp is the Ist completion time; the old postgres source used confirmation_time for both). recorded_at -> updated_at. /api/confirmations has NO status column, but every confirmation IS a completion -> status bound to the constant 'fertig' ({ const: 'fertig', smAttribute: 'status' }) so the OperationsResponse contract is complete and the cross-rule when-gates evaluate correctly (qty_shortfall gate status NE 'offen' runs; material_unavailable + late_delivery gate status EQ 'offen' stay dormant — the constant is load-bearing, the reconciler skips a missing when-attr). full_refresh change-detection (the REST API does not honour ?since — verified) — it-edge dedups via the per-id HWM on production_order_no. filter=null (full confirmation set per the cutover decision). machine_no/machine_id are null on the current confirmation feed (BDE bridge does not stamp the machine) — mapped for forward-compat; the ON_MACHINE edge resolves once the feed carries it. scrap_qty/good_qty/operator_id unmapped (not in the profile contract).
